_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Intensive Neonatal Care Package Babies with birthweight 1500-1799 g or Babies of any birthweight and at least one of the following conditions: • Need for mechanical ventilation for less than 24 hours or non-invasive respiratory support (CPAP, HFFNC) • Sepsis / pneumonia without complications • Hyperbilirubinemia requiring exchange transfusion • Seizures • Major congenital malformations (pre-surgical stabilization, not requiring ventilation) • Cholestasis significant enough requiring work up and in-hospital management • Congestive heart failure or shock Mother's stay and food in the hospital for breastfeeding, family centred care and (Kangaroo Mother Care) KMC is mandatory and included in the package rate
clinical_indications: Babies of any birthweight and at least one of the following conditions:
Need for mechanical ventilation for less than 24 hours or non-invasive respiratory support (CPAP, HFFNC)
Major congenital malformations (pre-surgical stabilization, not requiring ventilation)
Cholestasis significant enough requiring work up and in-hospital management
Mother's stay and food in the hospital for breastfeeding, family centred care and (Kangaroo Mother Care) KMC is mandatory and included in the package rate

clinical_thresholds.note: one of the primary criteria for package eligibility
for mechanical ventilation as a qualifying condition
one of the indications for exchange transfusion in hyperbilirubinemia
one of the indications for exchange transfusion in Rh iso-immunization
one of the indications for exchange transfusion in Rh iso-immunization
one of the indications for exchange transfusion

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of management
Birth weight (if condition is birthweight 1500-1799g)
Gestation age (if condition is birthweight 1500-1799g)
Ballard scoring for determining maturity (if condition is birthweight 1500-1799g)
Respiratory support - Silverman score need for Surfactant/Chest X-ray/CPAP/MV (if condition is birthweight 1500-1799g)
Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP) screening (if condition is birthweight 1500-1799g) - First ROP screening venue/date documented on discharge summary
Pulse oximetry (if condition is mechanical ventilation/non-invasive respiratory support)
Chest X-Ray (if condition is mechanical ventilation/non-invasive respiratory support, sepsis/pneumonia, congenital malformations, heart failure/shock)
Arterial Blood Gas (ABG) analysis (if condition is mechanical ventilation/non-invasive respiratory support, heart failure/shock)
Liver function test (if condition is hyperbilirubinemia, cholestasis)
Coomb's test (Direct) (if condition is hyperbilirubinemia)
Complete blood count (if condition is hyperbilirubinemia, heart failure/shock)
Blood grouping (mother and newborn) (if condition is hyperbilirubinemia)
Hearing assessment (BERA) (if condition is hyperbilirubinemia) - BERA screening planned before 3 months age with venue/date documented
Sr Electrolytes (if condition is seizures, heart failure/shock)
Blood Sugar (if condition is seizures, heart failure/shock)
Serum Calcium (if condition is seizures)
Septic Screen (if condition is sepsis/pneumonia, seizures, cholestasis)
Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) examination (if condition is seizures)
Neurosonogram (if condition is seizures, heart failure/shock)
Electroencephalogram (EEG) – based on availability (if condition is seizures)
Clinical Examination (if condition is major congenital malformations)
Clinical photograph (if condition is major congenital malformations)
Thyroid profile (if condition is cholestasis)
USG Abdomen (if condition is cholestasis)
Urine for bile pigments (if condition is cholestasis)
HIDA scan (based on the availability) (if condition is cholestasis)
Coagulation parameters (prothrombin time, activated partial thromboplastin time) (if condition is heart failure/shock)
Electrolytes, blood sugar, BUN/creatinine and urinalysis (if condition is heart failure/shock)
Functional Echocardiography (if condition is heart failure/shock)
Electrocardiogram (if condition is heart failure/shock)
Serum lactate (if condition is heart failure/shock)
Culture (if condition is heart failure/shock)
Cross matching and typing of blood (if condition is heart failure/shock)

claim.label: Detailed Procedure notes and indication (if any)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes including history, signs and symptoms, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission (refer the clinical criteria mentioned above)?
Was the neonate born at birth weight 1500-1799 grams documented?
Was the Silverman score/Chest X-ray/ABG documented to ventilatory support requirement?
Was sepsis screen/blood culture/Chest X-ray report confirm the diagnosis?
Was TSB recorded on normogram /neurodevelopmental signs/proven sepsis indicative of exchange transfusion?
Were the reports of Sr Electrolytes/Blood Sugar/Serum Calcium/Septic Screen/Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) examination/Neurosonogram submitted?
Was the patient hemodynamically stable (normal temperature, oxygenation, perfusion, blood sugar, HR, RR) documented?
Did the TSB report document elevated direct fraction?
Was ABG (metabolic acidosis)/lactate/Chest X-ray/2DECHO/urine output/hypotension/day of presentation/sudden development of cyanosis/type of shock documented?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Was the indication for procedure documented (optional)?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Birth weight not documented or outside the 1500-1799g range if that is the primary indication
Indication for ventilatory support not documented, or mechanical ventilation duration exceeds 24 hours without justification
Sepsis screen, blood culture, or Chest X-ray reports missing for sepsis/pneumonia cases
Indication for exchange transfusion not documented for hyperbilirubinemia cases
Required reports (Sr Electrolytes, Blood Sugar, Serum Calcium, Septic Screen, CSF examination, Neurosonogram) missing for seizure cases
Clinical photograph for major congenital malformations not indicative of surgery or ventilatory support was required
TSB report with elevated direct fraction not submitted for cholestasis
Investigations and clinical presentation do not confirm diagnosis of congestive heart failure/shock

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: DM/DNB/Equivalent (in Neonatology), referral to Pediatric Surgeon (Major Congenital Malformations)
clinical_key_pointers: Common causes for babies with birthweight 1500-1799 g: preterm labor/pre-labour rupture of membranes (chorioamnionitis), maternal illness (hypertension, diabetes, heart disease), pregnancy problems (placenta previa, placental abruption, cervical incompetence), multiple pregnancy/polyhydramnios

Common neonatal complications: Asphyxia necessitating resuscitation, Metabolic hypoglycemia, Mild respiratory distress, Feed intolerance / GERD, Sepsis, Anemia of prematurity, Neonatal Jaundice, Poor feeding, Hypothermia
Less common complications: infection, periventricular hemorrhage in the brain, bruising of the skin and patent ductus arteriosus
Care/Support: Feeding support (palladai or Nasogastric tube), Kangaroo mother Care, Thermal regulation, Nonnutritive suckling

Management for babies with birthweight 1500-1799 g: Treat underlying cause, Resuscitation if poor breathing at birth, Prevent hypothermia, Prevent hypoglycaemia, Start early feeds or IV 10% dextrose, Prevent apnoea, Monitor infant carefully and treat complications, Decide transfer to Level III care

NNPD defines respiratory distress as presence of any two of: RR >60/minute, Subcostal/intercostal recessions, Expiratory grunt/groaning. Also nasal flaring, suprasternal retractions, decreased air entry.
Grading severity of Respiratory Distress: Silverman Anderson score (preterm), Downe`s score (preterm and term)
Algorithm for initial management of respiratory distress (flowcharts provided)

Mechanical ventilation indications: Hypoxemia (PaO2 < 50 mmHg or O2 sat < 85%), Hypercarbia (PaCO2 < 60 mmHg and pH <7.2), Recurrent/severe apnea, Shallow/gasping irregular respiration, Failure of non-invasive support

CPAP Indications: Spontaneously breathing premature infant with respiratory distress, recurrent apneas not responding to medical management, post extubation, at-risk neonates for RDS, term neonates with respiratory distress and saturations <90% on hood oxygen, Apnea of prematurity, Term neonates with MAS, pneumonia, TTN

HFNC Indications: Post extubation support, aid in weaning from CPAP, alternative to CPAP in stable preterm neonates (for nasal trauma, nursing care, mother-infant bonding, kangaroo care, oral feeding), Apnea of prematurity

Neonatal sepsis definition: clinical signs/symptoms of septicemia, pneumonia or meningitis with pathogen isolation from sterile body fluids. Clinical sepsis: signs/symptoms of sepsis with negative blood/CSF culture.
Sepsis categories: Suspected sepsis (risk factors), Presumed/clinical sepsis (findings consistent with infection), Early onset sepsis (EOS) ≤ 72h, Late onset sepsis (LOS) > 72h

Hyperbilirubinemia requiring exchange transfusion: removes bilirubin and antibody coated erythrocytes, replaces with uncoated donor RBCs. Very rapid method for lowering dangerously high serum bilirubin.

Features of bilirubin encephalopathy (BE): Initial Phase (Lethargy, poor suck, hypotonia, high-pitched cry), Intermediate phase (irritability, variable tone, retrocollis/opisthotonos), Advanced phase (coma, absent suck, shrill cry, marked opisthotonos). BIND score helps identify early signs and severity.
Neonatal seizure definition: sudden paroxysmal depolarization of neurons resulting in transient alteration in sensory, motor, behavior or autonomic activity, with or without altered consciousness.
Seizure grouping: Epileptic seizures (with EEG changes), Non-epileptic seizures (without EEG correlate), EEG seizures (abnormal EEG activity without clinical correlation).
Important etiologies of neonatal seizures: HIE (38-48%), Neonatal Hypoglycaemia (3-7.5%), Hypocalcaemia (2.3-9%), CNS Infection (5.5-10.3%)

Major congenital malformations definition: abnormalities of body structure or function present at birth, of prenatal origin, medical/surgical/cosmetic significance, often require surgical repair or life threatening. (List of various malformations by body part provided on page 9)

Neonatal cholestasis (NC) definition: conjugated hyperbilirubinemia due to diminished bile flow. Conjugated hyperbilirubinemia: direct/conjugated bilirubin > 1.0 mg/dL if TSB <5.0 mg/dL, or >20% of TSB if TSB >5.0 mg/dL.
Cholestasis symptoms: Jaundice with dark yellow urine (with or without pale stools). Associated factors: Hepatosplenomegaly, Dehydration, Failure to thrive.
Diagnostic approach to Neonatal Cholestasis (flowchart provided).

Management for Cholestasis: treating underlying etiology. General Management: Medium chain Triglycerides (MCT) oil, Fat soluble Supplements (Vitamin A,D,E,K), Water soluble supplements (Twice RDA), Calcium, Phosphate, Zinc, Ursodeoxycholoic acid, Prophylactic phenobarbitone, Portal hypertension – propranolol, Pediatric Surgical consultation if required, End stage disease – Liver transplantation.
Shock definition: acute circulatory failure resulting in decreased tissue/organ perfusion, leading to depletion of oxygen/substrate in cells.

Shock phases: Compensated (pallor, tachycardia, cool peripheral skin, prolonged capillary refill time), Uncompensated (hypotension, very prolonged capillary refill time, tachycardia, cold skin, rapid breathing, reduced/absent urine output), Irreversible.
Non-specific signs of shock: lethargy, irritability, unresponsiveness to painful stimuli, generalized hypotonia, decreased deep tendon reflex.
Types of Shock (Causes provided on page 12): Cardiogenic, Hypovolemic, Distributive, Obstructive, Dissociative.
Suggested Treatment algorithm for Preterm and Term Newborns (flowcharts provided).
additional_information.fitness_criteria: For referral to Pediatric Surgery (Major Congenital Malformations): Hemodynamically stable, RR < 60 beats/min, Temperature (normothermia), Oxygenation (maintaining saturation in desired range), Perfusion (normal Capillary refill time), Blood sugar (Normoglycemia)
additional_information.referral_criteria: Decide whether the infant needs to be transferred to a level III care
contraindications: CPAP Contra-indications: Poor respiratory efforts, Recurrent or severe apnea requiring bag and mask ventilation, Congenital diaphragmatic hernia, Tracheoesophageal fistula, Choanal atresia, Cleft palate, Severe cardio-vascular instability, pH < 7.25 and PaCO2 > 60 mm of Hg
HFNC Contra-indications: Poor spontaneous efforts, Neonates with severe RDS, Recurrent or severe apnea requiring bag and mask ventilation, Congenital anomalies of the upper
